title: artemisia campestris subsp. pacifica
intro: Artemisia campestris subsp. pacifica is a subspecies of the sagebrush plant, native to the Pacific coast of North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off infections, due to the presence of compounds such as thujone and camphor. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, including relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and promoting wound healing. Artemisia campestris subsp. pacifica can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil for its medicinal and therapeutic uses.
